page: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with a high base clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance provides 2 vCPUs derived from a single physical core with 2 threads. It is equipped with 42.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it contains no GPUs.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and int8 operations, and cryptographic acceleration via VAES and SHA-NI. In benchmark evaluations, the high-frequency CPU delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2335.0 and strong single-threaded compression and decompression metrics. However, due to the physical constraint of having only one physical core, multi-core performance is limited, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the poor and weak categories respectively.

Despite the large 42.0 GB memory capacity, memory bandwidth benchmarks show some limitations, with large-block read and write speeds categorized as poor. Database operations and Redis SET performance also register in the weak category, reflecting the single-core bottleneck for parallelized workloads. For LLM inference, the server achieves an average text generation speed of 129.57 tokens per second on a small 135M model, but performance drops significantly on larger models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX2ms_v2 offers a specialized resource profile that pairs a high-frequency single physical core with a very dense memory allocation. This makes it a qualitative fit for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing, such as single-threaded legacy applications, memory-intensive text processing, or lightweight caching layers that benefit from a large memory footprint without high multi-core CPU demands.
description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core, 2 threads) operating at a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz, paired with 42.0 GB of RAM. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports premium storage. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2335.0 and strong single-threaded compression results. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are relatively weak due to the single-core hardware constraint. This server is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads, text processing, and lightweight database tasks that require a high memory-to-core ratio of 21.0 GB per core without demanding heavy parallel processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-optimized VM featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 2 vCPUs, and 42.0 GB of RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX2ms_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 2 vCPUs, 42.0 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual machine delivering strong single-core performance and an exceptional memory-to-vCPU ratio for specialized workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
High memory density of 42.0 GB RAM with 2 vCPUs
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and compression tests
No local storage included, but premium storage capable
Best suited for memory-bound, single-threaded applications and text processing
